title: Eating House License Registration

mainHeading: Eating House License Registration Online Service in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: An Eating House License is a compulsory requirement which is issued by the Licensing Police Commissioner of the respective state or city’s police department for operating in any place where food or beverages are supplied for consumption on the premises. This includes restaurants, cafes, dhabas, food joints, canteens, bars etc. As per the Delhi Police Act and similar laws in other states, obtaining an Eating House License is crucial before commencing food service operations.

Professionals Law Private Limited offers comprehensive assistance and support in obtaining Eating House Licenses across India, particularly in the NCT of Delhi, state of Haryana and Uttar Pradesh. We provide full assistance in documentation, coordination with police authorities and compliance with all the legal requirements.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: Running a food business without an Eating House License is illegal and may result in punitive actions or even closure. It ensures that the establishment maintains public order and complies with local civic and safety norms. This license is also a prerequisite for obtaining other business permits, such as a liquor license or entertainment license and is necessary for building customer trust and brand credibility.

body.title: Legal Authorization to Operate
body.description: An Eating House License ensures your establishment is recognized by local law enforcement, reducing the risk of raids, fines or shutdowns.

body.title: Builds Credibility with Authorities and Customers
body.description: Licensed food businesses are more likely to earn the trust of customers and regulatory bodies, which is essential for long-term sustainability.

body.title: Pre-requisite for Other Licenses
body.description: If you plan to serve alcohol or apply for music/entertainment permissions, the Eating House License is a must-have document.

body.title: Boosts Business Opportunities
body.description: Having the license allows you to participate in food festivals, collaborate with food delivery platforms and qualify for government contracts or subsidies.

criteria: The applicant must be a registered business entity (Proprietorship/Partnership/LLP/ Company).
The premises must be authorized for commercial use.
FSSAI registration/license must already be obtained.
The applicant must have fire safety clearance and other relevant permissions depending on the size and nature of the eating house.
Police verification of the owner/applicant is mandatory.

documents: Trade License from Local Municipal Body
Fire Safety Certificate (for seating capacity above a certain threshold)
Site Plan and Layout of the Premises
Identity and Address Proof of Owner/Directors
Undertaking for Installation of CCTV Cameras and Fire Extinguishers
Company Incorporation Certificate or Partnership Deed

body.description: Collect all required documents, including site plans, photographs and regulatory licenses.
Submit the application to the Licensing Police Commissioner’s portal along with the prescribed fees.
Local police conduct verification of premises and applicant background.
Inspection of safety measures like fire equipment and CCTV installation.
Upon successful verification, the Eating House License is granted within the stipulated time frame.

body.title: Operating Without License
body.description: Fines ranging from ₹5,000 to ₹50,000 depending on jurisdiction.

body.title: Temporary Closure
body.description: Temporary closure or sealing of the premises by local authorities.

body.title: Legal Prosecution
body.description: Legal prosecution under relevant municipal or police acts.

body.title: Repeat Offenses
body.description: In some cases, criminal charges for repeat offenders or public endangerment.

body.title: Delhi
body.description: Approximately 25 to 30 working days after submission and police verification.

body.title: Haryana
body.description: Around 30 to 40 working days, subject to local police processing.

body.title: Uttar Pradesh
body.description: Typically, 30 to 45 working days, depending on district-level clearance and inspections.

body.question: What is an Eating House License?
body.answer: It is a police-issued permit allowing you to run an establishment where food or beverages are served to customers for on-site consumption.

body.question: Is it mandatory for all food businesses?
body.answer: Yes, if your business provides a place for eating or dining (like restaurants, cafés, food courts), this license is compulsory.

body.question: Can I apply for it before opening the restaurant?
body.answer: Yes. You should apply once you have your FSSAI license and local municipal trade license.

body.question: How long is the license valid?
body.answer: The Eating House License is usually valid for 3 years, after which it needs to be renewed.

body.question: Do I need this license for takeaway or cloud kitchens?
body.answer: No, it is generally required only if there is a sitting/dining area where customers can eat on the premises.

body.question: Can Professionals Law help with inspection preparation?
body.answer: Absolutely. We guide you through police verification, documentation and ensure your premises meet all compliance standards.

body.question: What happens if I operate without this license?
body.answer: You may face fines, closure and in some cases, legal action or imprisonment under local municipal or police laws.

body.question: Is CCTV installation mandatory?
body.answer: Yes, installation of functional CCTV systems on the premises is a standard compliance requirement.

body.question: Does Professionals Law provide this service pan-India?
body.answer: Yes, but we specialize in fast-tracked approvals in Delhi, Haryana and Uttar Pradesh through our regional experts.
